current_record: 30-3 (overall) — final, season ended April 2026; NCAA Division III National Champions

head_coach.name: Marcus Kahn
head_coach.background: Completed 12th season as Mary Washington head coach. 2026 Glenn Robinson National Coach of the Year. Coast-to-Coast Athletic Conference Coaching Staff of the Year (2026). Hanford, California native. Led Eagles to their first NCAA Division III National Championship in program history.

roster.notes: Hometown listed as Jacksonville FL but school as Hayfield (Fairfax, VA)

roster.name: Preston White
roster.notes: From Gainesville HS; height not listed on official roster

roster.name: Colin Mitchell
roster.notes: From Benedictine Prep. Hit buzzer-beating putback to win 2026 NCAA D3 National Championship vs Emory

recent_games.tournament: NCAA D3 National Championship Game (Colin Mitchell buzzer-beating putback)

postseason_2025_26.result: NCAA Division III National Champions — defeated Emory 75-73 on April 5, 2026 on Colin Mitchell buzzer-beating putback. First national title in program history.

sources.notes: Marcus Kahn Glenn Robinson National Coach of the Year

verification_notes: MAJOR ACCOMPLISHMENT: Mary Washington won the 2026 NCAA Division III Men's Basketball National Championship on April 5, 2026, defeating Emory 75-73 on a Colin Mitchell buzzer-beating putback — the first national title in program history. Final record 30-3. Marcus Kahn completed his 12th season as head coach and won 2026 Glenn Robinson National Coach of the Year. UMW competes in Coast-to-Coast Athletic Conference (note: historically was in Capital Athletic Conference; the conference rebranded/expanded). Conference record left out of overall format because some sources show 0-0 conference (likely due to data source treating C2C games differently or scheduling); the school's official A-10 records page should be cross-referenced before publishing any 'conference record' claim. Venue name 'William M. Anderson Center' is the official UMW basketball venue but capacity could not be confirmed from authoritative sources — left null. Athletic director Patrick Catullo confirmed (promoted from interim in February 2020). D3 coverage is sparse — recommend manual verification of any player names, heights, or jersey numbers before publication. Owen Pottenburgh hometown listed as Jacksonville FL on official roster but his high school is Hayfield (Fairfax, VA) — possible data inconsistency on UMW roster page; verify before use. Preston White height not listed. 2026-27 schedule TBD.
